Description: Phishing-driven credential compromise (T-01) leading to unauthorized access to Patient Records DB (A-01).
Ransomware deployment on HIS servers (A-02) via T-02, disrupting clinical operations and patient safety.
Cloud misconfiguration in AWS production VPC (A-07) exposes billing data (A-08) (T-03).
SQL injection / access control flaw in patient portal (T-10) exposes Patient Records DB (A-01) via AWS prod (A-07).
Supply-chain compromise via HL7 lab integration (A-12) enabling lateral movement (T-06).
Compromise of legacy diagnostic imaging device (A-06) used as network pivot point (T-07).
Credential-stuffing attack against VPN gateway (A-09) results in unauthorized remote access (T-09).
Accidental data exposure via misdirected email or open file share containing PHI (A-01, A-04) (T-04).
Malicious insider exfiltrates patient records (A-01) prior to offboarding (T-05).
Extended outage of production data center / cloud region affecting A-02, A-07, A-11 (T-08).
Wireless eavesdropping or rogue AP on clinical Wi-Fi (A-13) intercepts unencrypted traffic (T-12).
Vendor/contractor with excessive privileged access introduces malware during maintenance (A-02, A-03) (T-11).
